content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission

P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • • Administration • • • Executive/Legal • • • Enforcement • • • Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5662          322-5660          322-5901          322-6441          322-6444


December 19, 1979


Mr. Emil Abate
Hayward Citizens for Rent Relief
27917 Biscayne Avenue
Hayward, California  94544

Dear Mr. Abate:

You have written requesting information about filing dates in
connection with a petition drive to qualify a ballot measure.

The statement due following the attempt to qualify the measure
is due 35 days after the deadline for filing petitions or the
date of notification that the measure has either qualified or
failed to qualify, whichever date is earlier. It is possible,
therefore, that the date used to calculate when the statement
is due will be earlier than January 24. If so, the report will
be due prior to February 28, with a closing date seven days
prior to the filing date.

The first pre-election statement for an election to be held
April 8, 1980, is February 28, 1980, not February 27. The
closing date for this statement will be February 25, 1980.
This statement, in your case, will cover the period from the
closing date of the qualification drive statement above (as
late as February 21, 1980) through February 25, 1980.

I hope this letter answers your question. If the requirement
is still unclear, you may call me at 916/322-5662.

Sincerely,

[signature]
JOHN GIANSELLO
Political Reform Analyst

Hayward Citizens for Rent Relief
27917 Biscayne Ave.
Hayward, Ca. 94544

December 12, 1979


FPPC
1100 Kay St.
Sacramento, Ca. 95814

To whom it may concern;

The Hayward Citizens for Rent Relief supported Measure One
(City of Hayward Initiative) in the July 17, 1979 special election.
Since then we have been circulating petitions for an initiative
to be be put on the Hayward Municipal Election Ballot of April
8, 1980. Deadline for submitting petitions is January 24, which
when we will be turning in our petitions. My question is sim-
ply this: When is our campaign statement due?

As I read the law we one report due February 28 with a
closing date of February 21 and another due February 27 with a
closing date of Feb 24. It seems to work out this way because
the filing dates for after submitting petitions and the first
pre-election statements fall on the same week.


Cordially,

Emil Abate
Emil Abate
Hayward Citizens for Rent Relief
